quests.title: Active Quests
quests.comingSoonDesc: The quest system is currently under development. Soon you will be able to complete tasks, earn rewards, and discover secrets of the reservoirs!
quests.noAvailable: No quests available at the moment.

items.a: Select a location on the map (initial one is 'Forest Lake'), pick a casting spot, set up your gear, and hit the 'Cast' button. When the bobber goes underwater or you feel strong resistance (for spinning), strike and start reeling!

The game uses accelerated time: 1 real second equals approximately 20 game seconds. The world stays alive even when you're offline — the system calculates elapsed time and updates weather upon your return.
Weather changes dynamically. Rain and clouds increase fish activity, especially predators. Clear skies make fish more cautious but allow lures to flash brighter.

items.q: What is special about the Forest Lake?
items.a: It's a natural starting location. You'll mostly find small to medium fish like Crucian, Roach, and Perch. There is a slight water current, and occasionally you might snag some trash from the bottom.

items.q: How is the Fish Farm different?
items.a: It's an artificial reservoir with crystal clear water and high fish density. It holds valuable species like Carp, Grass Carp, and large predators. There is no current, and the chance of catching trash here is very low.

items.q: What should I know about the Pasture Canal?
items.a: The canal has a steady current and a deep central channel. It's the best place for Asp and Bream. Be careful with casting near the steep banks, as they can be tricky.
This is the base probability that a fish will be interested in your bait. A high value represents maximum initial interest.

items.q: How does weight affect the game?
items.a: Weight determines the fish's price in the shop and its resistance during the fight. Heavier fish are harder to pull in but much more valuable.
It defines how active a fish is at different times. High activity indicates the perfect time to fish, while low activity means the fish will mostly ignore your bait.
Aggression is the force with which a fish pulls and jerks the line. High aggression increases the risk of snapping your line if you're not careful.
Stamina determines how long a fish can fight actively before getting tired. High stamina fish require more patience during reeling and easily exhaust your gear.
This represents how quickly a fish approaches the bait once it notices it in the water.
Yes, the fear parameter determines how easily a fish is spooked by loud splashes or incorrect lure movement.
Float — classic fishing where you see bites on a bobber. Feeder — bottom fishing with a groundbait cage. Spinning — active fishing with artificial lures requiring constant retrieval.

items.q: How does the tension system work?
items.a: The tension bar shows the load on your gear. Red zone means risk of breaking. Empty zone means risk of fish escaping. Keep tension in the yellow-green zone.

items.q: How do lures affect predator catches?
items.a: Different predators have specific preferences. For example, Pike loves wobblers and spoons, while Zander and Perch respond much better to vibrotails.
Perch is an active predator that bites best in the morning and evening. Vibrotails work great. Use light gear and active retrieval.

items.q: What is the best tactic for Pike?
items.a: Pike is exceptionally aggressive and makes sharp jerks. It takes well to wobblers or spoons. Use strong fishing line and avoid pulling by force when it's actively fighting — let it tire out first.

items.q: How to catch a big Carp?
items.a: Carp are most active during evening and night and boast incredible stamina. Use a feeder rig combined with 'Pea Porridge' groundbait and cast corn or dough as bait.
It's a strictly nocturnal hunter. Fish for it at night in deep waters using vibrotails, to which it responds incredibly well.

items.q: How to catch the legendary Catfish?
items.a: Catfish are nocturnal and are by far the strongest fighters in the game. You'll need the most powerful gear available and should target the deepest holes using live bait or large vibrotails.

items.q: Why am I catching so many Ruffe?
items.a: Ruffe has immense curiosity and bites almost always. If you want to avoid it and target bigger fish, consider changing your spot, fishing depth or bait type.
It's a very shy and cautious fish. It requires careful casting and high endurance during the fight due to its natural strength and stamina.

items.q: What is the secret to catching Asp?
items.a: Asp is a fast-water predator. Use spoons or wobblers and retrieve them quickly in the upper water layers. It strikes hard, so be ready!

items.q: How to target Bream in the canal?
items.a: Bream loves the deep channel at the bottom. Use a feeder rig with 'Vanillin Mix' and target depths between 1-2 meters.

items.q: How to find the mysterious Eel?
items.a: Eel is strictly nocturnal. Fish for it at night in the canal using worms or live bait on the bottom. It's incredibly strong despite its shape.
Mixtures create an 'attraction zone' where fish swim more actively. It doesn't pull them like a magnet but keeps them within the scent radius longer.
It's perfect for quickly attracting schooling 'white' fish like Roach, Crucian, and Ruffe. Great for beginners.
This is a heavy mixture for trophy fish like Carp and Grass Carp. It helps 'filter out' smaller fish so you can wait for a real monster.
This is a strong attractant for predators. It lures Zander, Perch, and Catfish out of their ambushes to your hook.

items.q: Do rods and reels wear out?
items.a: Yes, fighting fish reduces durability. Use a Repair Kit to restore them, otherwise your gear might break at the worst moment.

items.q: Why different types of fishing line?
items.a: Different lines have varying strength and visibility. Braided cord is the strongest, while fluorocarbon is nearly invisible to fish.
Target Carp or predators (Pike, Zander) as they are worth more than small fish. Also, keep an eye on your keepnet capacity.

gear.title: My Equipment
gear.notAvailableForSpinning: This section is not available while using a spinning rod.
gear.equipment_guide: In the Gear menu, you can configure your fishing tackle. There are 3 fishing styles available: float, bottom, and spinning. Selecting a spinning rod will automatically equip a spinning lure (and vice versa). For bottom fishing, use a classic rod and choose a feeder rig in the hooks section.

repairModal.title: Select item to repair
repairModal.empty: All your gear is in perfect condition!

portrait.hint: The game works only in landscape mode

perch.name: Perch
perch.description: A clever predator with distinctive stripes. Found near vegetation.

pike.name: Pike
pike.description: An apex predator of freshwater. Prefers deep water ambushes.

carp.name: Carp
carp.description: Powerful bottom dweller known for its incredible resistance.

crucian.name: Crucian
crucian.description: Resilient fish that survives in small ponds. Bites cautiously.

roach.name: Roach
roach.description: Schooling fish found in various depths. Very common.

zander.name: Zander
zander.description: Sleek deep water predator with excellent low-light vision.

ruffe.name: Ruffe
ruffe.description: Small spiny fish, relative of perch. Often annoys with small nibbles.

catfish.name: Catfish
catfish.description: King of the depths. A nocturnal giant hiding in the darkest holes.

grass_carp.name: Grass Carp
grass_carp.description: Large herbivorous resident. Fights fiercely when hooked.

american_catfish.name: American Catfish
american_catfish.description: A small but extremely active and curious bottom dweller. Often bites in groups.

asp.name: Asp
asp.description: A lightning-fast predator of the upper water layers. Known for powerful surface strikes.

bream.name: Bream
bream.description: A flat-bodied bottom dweller. Very cautious and prefers deep, quiet areas.

crayfish.name: Crayfish
crayfish.description: A bottom-dwelling crustacean. Usually a sign of clean water.

eel.name: Eel
eel.description: A mysterious, snake-like fish. Strictly nocturnal and very strong.

gudgeon.name: Gudgeon
gudgeon.description: A small, schooling fish that loves sandy bottoms and clear water.

silver_carp.name: Silver Carp
silver_carp.description: A huge plankton eater. Can jump out of the water when startled.

tench.name: Tench
tench.description: The 'doctor' fish of the reservoir. Very shy and lives in thick weeds.

weatherfish.name: Weatherfish
weatherfish.description: Can predict weather changes. Becomes very active before a storm.

worm.name: Worm
worm.description: Classic and reliable bait. Suitable for many fish species.

maggot.name: Maggot
maggot.description: Excellent for roach and smaller competition fish.

bread.name: Bread
bread.description: Simple bait for crucian and carp.

corn.name: Corn
corn.description: Attracts big carps and grass carps.

dough.name: Dough
dough.description: Soft bait, fish love to nibble it.

live_bait.name: Live Bait
live_bait.description: Small fish, attracts pike and zander.

vanillin.name: Vanillin Mix
vanillin.description: Sweet aroma that quickly gathers schooling fish.

peas.name: Pea Porridge
peas.description: Heavy bottom mixture. Best for big carp.

dried_blood.name: Dried Blood
dried_blood.description: Strong scent that drives predators crazy.

forest_lake.name: Forest Lake
forest_lake.description: A cozy lake surrounded by ancient trees. Varied depth, rich vegetation.

fish_farm.name: Fish Farm
fish_farm.description: Artificial reservoir for breeding. Even bottom, clear water, high fish density.

pasture_canal.name: Pasture Canal
pasture_canal.description: A long irrigation canal passing through lush green pastures. Steady current and steep grassy banks.

rod_starter.name: Bamboo Stick
rod_starter.description: Strong but very basic bamboo rod.

hook_steel.name: Steel Hook
hook_steel.description: Sharp and very resistant to bending.

hook_barbed.name: Laser Cut Barbed
hook_barbed.description: Fish almost never escape from this hook.

hook_feeder_basic.name: Simple Feeder Rig
hook_feeder_basic.description: Classic bottom sinker with a hook. Always stays on bottom.

hook_feeder_pro.name: Professional Feeder
hook_feeder_pro.description: Long-range mixture cage with extra sharp hook.

line_thread.name: Cotton Thread
line_thread.description: Breaks just by looking at it.

reel_basic.name: Spinning Reel
reel_basic.description: Does the job for small fish.

rod_spinning_basic.name: Starter Spinning
rod_spinning_basic.description: Lightweight rod designed for active lure work.

rod_spinning_pro.name: Graphite Master
rod_spinning_pro.description: Great balance between sensitivity and strength.

echo_sounder.name: Echo Sounder
echo_sounder.description: Electronic device that shows fish presence and depth.

repair_kit.name: Repair Kit
repair_kit.description: Restores durability to damaged rods and reels.

tangled_line.name: Tangled Line
tangled_line.description: A mess of broken fishing line.

driftwood.name: Driftwood
driftwood.description: A piece of wood soaked in water.

bottomInfo.title: Project Information
bottomInfo.text:
WELCOME TO FISHORIA — a next-generation web-based fishing simulator that combines classic gameplay with deep technical simulation. We've created an immersive world where every cast matters and every fish has its own character.

GAMEPLAY EXPERIENCE
Fishoria offers a comprehensive fishing experience featuring multiple fishing styles: Float, Feeder, and Spinning. Manage your equipment carefully — from selecting the right hook size and line type (Mono, Fluoro, or Braid) to choosing the perfect bait for your target species. Success requires mastering the tension system: keep the pressure in the sweet spot to tire the fish without snapping your line or allowing it to escape.

ADVANCED FISH AI
The heart of Fishoria is our sophisticated Fish AI system. Instead of simple random encounters, our fish are autonomous agents powered by complex steering behaviors and environmental awareness:
• NATURAL MOVEMENT: Fish use Simplex noise-driven wandering and flocking behaviors to navigate the underwater world realistically.
• DYNAMIC LIFESTYLES: Every fish follows its own schedule, including migration to preferred depth zones, rest periods, and active hunting phases.
• ENVIRONMENTAL SENSITIVITY: Fish behavior changes dynamically with the weather and time of day. Predators become more aggressive during rain and low-light conditions, while prey fish seek shelter.
• REACTIVE INTELLIGENCE: Fish react to your presence. A loud splash might attract a hungry Pike but will scare away a cautious Crucian.
• BITING STRATEGIES: Some fish are cautious 'nibblers' that test your patience, while others are aggressive 'strikers' that hit the bait with force.

Explore beautifully rendered reservoirs, upgrade your gear, hunt for legendary trophies, and climb the global leaderboard. Whether you're a casual player or a hardcore angler, Fishoria offers a peaceful yet challenging escape into the world of fishing.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.text: Your privacy is important to us. We collect minimal data required for game operation, including username and basic progress. We do not sell your data to third parties. By playing Fishoria, you agree to our simple and fair privacy standards. We use cookies to maintain your session and ensure a smooth gaming experience. All collected data is used exclusively to improve the game and manage your account achievements and leaderboard standing.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.text: Welcome to Fishoria. By using this application, you agree to stay respectful, avoid exploits or cheating, and use the service as intended. We reserve the right to manage accounts that violate our community standards. All game assets and code are property of Fishoria developers. Our services are provided 'as is' without warranties of any kind. Continued use of the game manifests your complete and binding acceptance of these simple terms and any future modifications that may be published here.
